title: Missing values during insert
summary: Learn how SQL handles missing values during INSERT operations, including the use of NULL and DEFAULT values in the inventory_items table.

sketchBlocks.title: How SQL Handles Missing Values in INSERT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When inserting data into a table, you don't always have to provide a value for every column. If you omit a column in your INSERT statement, SQL will use the column's DEFAULT value if one is defined, or NULL if the column allows it. However, if a column is defined as NOT NULL and has no DEFAULT, you must provide a value, or the statement will fail.

For example, in the `inventory_items` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE inventory_items (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,
  price REAL NOT NULL,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
  is_test INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  notes TEXT NULL,
  last_updated TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '2026-02-01'
);
```

If you insert a row without specifying `status`, it will default to `'draft'`. If you omit `notes`, it will be set to NULL. But if you omit `name`, the insert will fail because `name` is NOT NULL and has no default.

sketchBlocks.title: INSERT Examples with NULL and DEFAULT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Let's see some INSERT examples:

```sql
-- Omitting 'status' and 'notes'
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES (7, 'Sample Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.00, 0, '2026-02-07');
```

Here, `status` will be set to its default `'draft'`, and `notes` will be NULL.

```sql
-- Explicitly inserting NULL for 'notes'
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES (8, 'Demo Magnet', 'Decor', 2.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-08');
```

You can insert NULL into columns that allow it, like `notes`.

quizDraft.title: Insert with omitted DEFAULT and NULL columns
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert a new row into `inventory_items` with the following values:
- id: 9
- name: 'Test Notebook'
- category: 'Stationery'
- price: 5.00
- is_test: 1
- last_updated: '2026-02-09'

Do NOT specify values for `status` or `notes`. Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Omit columns that have a DEFAULT or allow NULL. List only the columns you want to set.

help.concept: When you omit a column in an INSERT statement, SQL uses the column's DEFAULT value if defined, or NULL if allowed.
help.hint_1: List only the columns you want to set in the INSERT column list.
help.hint_2: Omitting 'status' will use its default, and omitting 'notes' will set it to NULL.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES (9, 'Test Notebook', 'Stationery', 5.00, 1, '2026-02-09');

quizDraft.title: Insert with explicit NULL value
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert a new row into `inventory_items` with these values:
- id: 10
- name: 'Sample Pen'
- category: 'Stationery'
- price: 1.50
- status: 'active'
- is_test: 0
- notes: NULL
- last_updated: '2026-02-10'

Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Use NULL for the notes column in your VALUES list.

help.concept: You can explicitly insert NULL into columns that allow it, like 'notes'.
help.hint_1: Include all columns in your INSERT column list.
help.hint_2: Write NULL (without quotes) for the notes value.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES (10, 'Sample Pen', 'Stationery', 1.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10');

quizDraft.title: Insert that fails due to missing NOT NULL column
quizDraft.prompt:
Try to insert a row into `inventory_items` with these values:
- id: 11
- category: 'Decor'
- price: 8.00
- status: 'active'
- is_test: 0
- last_updated: '2026-02-11'

Do NOT specify a value for `name`. Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Omitting a NOT NULL column with no default will cause an error.

help.concept: If you omit a NOT NULL column that has no DEFAULT, the INSERT will fail.
help.hint_1: Try writing the INSERT statement without the 'name' column.
help.hint_2: Notice that 'name' is NOT NULL and has no default, so the statement will not work.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, category, price, status, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES (11, 'Decor', 8.00, 'active', 0, '2026-02-11');

quizDraft.title: Which columns can be omitted in an INSERT?
quizDraft.prompt: Which columns in the `inventory_items` table can you safely omit from an INSERT statement (when using a column list)? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Look for columns that have a DEFAULT or allow NULL.

help.concept: Columns with a DEFAULT value or that allow NULL can be omitted from an INSERT statement.
help.hint_1: Check the table definition for DEFAULT values and NULL constraints.
help.hint_2: Columns like 'status', 'is_test', and 'notes' can be omitted because they have a DEFAULT or allow NULL.

quizDraft.title: Default value for omitted column
quizDraft.prompt: If you omit the `status` column in an INSERT into `inventory_items`, what value will it have?
quizDraft.hint: Check the DEFAULT value for the 'status' column in the table definition.

help.concept: When a column with a DEFAULT is omitted in an INSERT, SQL uses the DEFAULT value.
help.hint_1: Look at the CREATE TABLE statement for the default value of 'status'.
help.hint_2: The default is a text value set in single quotes.
